title: this should not be exposed from constructors
description: "this" should not be exposed from constructorsAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode Smellmulti-threadingsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?In single-threaded environments, the use ofthisin constructors is normal, and expected. But in multi-threaded environments, it could expose partially-constructed objects to other threads, and should be used with caution.The classic example is a class with astaticlist of its instances. If the constructor storesthisin the list, another thread could access the object before it’s fully-formed.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: 'this' should not be exposed from constructors - it may expose a partially-constructed object to other threads

logic.detection_conditions: Constructor that passes 'this' to external code (static collections, events, callbacks)
logic.triggering_conditions: Constructor body contains 'this' passed to collections (.Add(this)), events (handler(this,)), or callbacks
never_trigger_on: Factory method patterns (Create method that adds to collection after construction)
logic.reasoning: In multi-threaded environments, exposing 'this' from a constructor can allow other threads to access a partially-constructed object, leading to race conditions and bugs. Use factory methods instead.

should_not_trigger: public static MyClass Create() { var instance = new MyClass(); instances.Add(instance); return instance; }
public void Initialize(EventHandler handler) { handler(this, EventArgs.Empty); }
